message.content: To avoid repeated tool loops, agents must implement robust state tracking and early-stopping mechanisms. First, maintain a history of executed tool calls. Before invoking a tool, check if it has already been called in the current context with identical parameters; if so, skip execution to prevent infinite recursion. Second, use a "max retries" counter. If the agent fails to progress after a set number of attempts, force a termination or fallback to a human-in-the-loop review. Third, validate tool outputs. If the response is empty, malformed, or identical to previous iterations, treat it as a failure state rather than a signal to retry. Finally, enhance prompt engineering by explicitly instructing the model to halt if it detects redundancy or lack of progress. Combining programmatic constraints with semantic checks ensures agents break out of loops efficiently, maintaining system stability and resource integrity.
